content: Do you ever wonder why we have different seasons like winter, spring, summer, and fall? It's because of the way the Earth moves! The Earth rotates on its side as it goes around the sun, which means that different parts of the planet get different amounts of sunlight throughout the year. When the part of the Earth where you live is tilted towards the sun, you get more sunlight and it's warmer, which is why we have summer. When it's tilted away from the sun, you get less sunlight and it's colder, which is why we have winter. As the Earth keeps moving, the amount of sunlight changes, and that's why we have spring and fall too! It's like the Earth is dancing around the sun, and that's what makes our seasons change. Isn't that cool?
